title: What is Life-as-Code
problem: Visitors see a portfolio of features but have no context for what life-as-code is or why it matters.
analysis: A short explainer section rendered above the feature list that explains what life-as-code is, why provenance matters, and how to get started. The section has three parts: an intro/body block with prose paragraphs and a CTA; an ecosystem grid showing all six tools in the LAC toolchain. The CTA block provides an `npx @majeanson/lac init` command and a 'view on GitHub →' link so visitors can immediately try the workflow.

decisions.decision: Inline section in the single-page layout rather than a separate /about route
decisions.rationale: The explainer is short enough that routing adds friction — scrolling into the features is the natural reading flow.
alternativesConsidered: Modal or popover — hides content from skimmers

decisions.decision: About component imported into App.tsx above the feature list
decisions.rationale: Visitors read the explanation before encountering the features it describes.
alternativesConsidered: Below the feature list — too late; visitors hit cards before understanding them

decisions.decision: about-section styled with accent left-border matching the design system
decisions.rationale: Visually distinguishes the explainer from feature cards while staying within the same token vocabulary.
alternativesConsidered: Plain prose, no border — loses visual separation from feature list

lineage.spawnReason: The root feature needs to explain its own context to visitors who don't know what LAC is.

implementation: About.tsx is a purely presentational React component with no props or data fetching, rendered as a <section> above the feature list using about-label/about-body layout. The body block contains four paragraphs (lead, what LAC is, the showcase self-reference, toolchain description) followed by an about-cta div with a styled <code> command and an <a> link to GitHub. The ecosystem grid maps a hardcoded array of six items — @majeanson/lac (npm), lac-lens (VS Code Marketplace), lac-mcp (GitHub), lifeascode (live demo), feature-schema (GitHub), lac-lsp (GitHub) — into ecosystem-card elements, each with a description and a working external link. All links use target='_blank' rel='noreferrer'. All styles reference CSS custom properties from feat-2026-003.
knownLimitations: All URLs are hardcoded in the ecosystem array — any href change requires a code edit
No i18n support — all copy is English-only JSX
No custom focus indicator on .about-link beyond browser defaults
Ecosystem item descriptions are not driven by feature.json — if a tool's scope changes, the About card won't update automatically
successCriteria: The explainer section renders above the feature list on page load, uses the app's design tokens, shows the npx init command in a code block, and all eight external links (6 ecosystem + GitHub CTA + npm CLI) open the correct destinations in a new tab.

statusHistory.reason: About.tsx significantly expanded: added npx CTA block, GitHub link, new toolchain paragraph, and all 6 ecosystem items now have working hrefs (lac-mcp, feature-schema, lac-lsp were previously null). Implementation and successCriteria describe 2 links; there are now 8+.

annotations.author: lac advance
